title: Why is the final movie shin evangelion When the first three were evangelion shin
display_title: Why is the final movie shin evangelion When the first three were evangelion shin
wikitext:
[]: Why is the final movie shin evangelion? When the first three were evangelion shin             Quote Post by Thesufferingpumpkin » Sun Dec 27, 2020 12:38 pm Just always wondered, the first 3 films carry the title Evangerion Shin Gekijōban But the final film is Shin Evangerion Gekijōban

[]: Re: Why is the final movie shin evangelion? When the first three were evangelion shin             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Sun Dec 27, 2020 12:58 pm For the time being, it's still unknown. But it should be noted that the kanji for New (新) and True (真) can both be read as "Shin". The ambiguity of Shin being written in katakana (シン) as well as its placement in Shin Eva's title makes it hard to guess its true meaning, unlike Shin Gekijouban (新劇場版) where the Shin kanji obviously means "new" and to represent the technical status of Rebuild as a new, separate production from NGE. However, other Anno projects like Shin Godzilla and Shin Ultraman -where both have the same シン・[Insert IP Name] title treatment- have the basic idea of returning to the true (真) roots of each respective franchise they belonged, while providing a new (新) and fresh take at the same time. So it's not impossible that シン in here represents both the True and New aspects of the film. Whether this still applies or not with the massive changes we've seen throughout the years, older interviews mentioned Anno's desire for Rebuild to be the Evangelion that he really wanted to make without all the budget constraints and stuff, in a sense it's Anno's "true Evangelion", while still being an entirely new production altogether.

[]: Re: Why is the final movie shin evangelion? When the first three were evangelion shin             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Sun Dec 27, 2020 1:11 pm In addition, "shin" creates a contrast to the word "kyu" (as the Japanese speak the letter "Q", present in 3.0 title), which can mean "old" represented by the kanji 『旧』. Another point, is that the previous titles used an obsolete kana 『ヱ』 (we) in the name of the franchise. Now, with "shin" (シ ン) in first place, we have the return of kana 『エ』(e) as we always had in NGE Japanese title. ヱ ヴァンゲリ ヲ ン ( we vangeri wo n) エ ヴァンゲリ オ ン ( e vangeri o n) EDIT: the use of "wo" in katakana is rare. Like we see in Ka wo ru (カ ヲ ル) name. Another observation: kanji represent ideas, while syllabaries hiragana and katakana represent only sounds. Then "shin" can mean other words in Japanese, creating the ambiguity cited by GhostlyOcam.

[]: Re: Why is the final movie shin evangelion? When the first three were evangelion shin             Quote Post by GhostlyOcam » Sun Dec 27, 2020 1:26 pm Yup, that too. They're being very playful with the Japanese titles of these films, it's easy to miss since it requires a certain level of understanding of the language. I find both the JP and English titles to be charming and unique for different reasons, although I'm almost exclusively call the last film Shin Eva because it's less mouthful to spell and took less time to type than "3.0+1.0".

[]: Re: Why is the final movie shin evangelion? When the first three were evangelion shin             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Sun Dec 27, 2020 1:30 pm GhostlyOcam wrote: I'm almost exclusively call the last film Shin Eva because it's less mouthful to spell and took less time to type than "3.0+1.0".

[]: Re: Why is the final movie shin evangelion? When the first three were evangelion shin             Quote Post by Joseki » Sun Dec 27, 2020 1:53 pm Because Hideaki Anno

[]: Re: Why is the final movie shin evangelion? When the first three were evangelion shin             Quote Post by FXArmaros98 » Tue Dec 29, 2020 9:54 am Joseki wrote: Because Hideaki Anno

[]: Re: Why is the final movie shin evangelion? When the first three were evangelion shin             Quote Post by andreh » Tue Dec 29, 2020 10:12 am Everybody gangsta until the title " Evangelion 3.333+.+++1.111 " shows up
